title: CLI entry point and command routing

story.i_want_to: type commands like `todo add` or `todo list` and have the app route to the right action
story.so_that: I have a consistent and intuitive interface for all operations

rationale: A clean CLI entry point with proper command routing is the backbone of the app. It determines the user experience for every interaction. Getting this right early means all future commands plug in easily.

acceptance_criteria.description: The app is invoked via `python todo.py <command> [args]`.
Running `python todo.py` with no arguments prints the help/usage message to stdout and exits with code 0.
Running `python todo.py unknowncommand` prints 'Error: Unknown command: unknowncommand' to stderr, followed by the usage message to stdout, and exits with code 1.
The app returns exit code 0 on success and exit code 1 on error.
Storage is initialized before any command handler is called.
All success/informational output goes to stdout. All error output goes to stderr.
Running `todo help` prints the same usage message as running `todo` with no arguments, and exits with code 0.
Running `todo --help` or `todo -h` prints the same usage message and exits with code 0.
Running `todo --version` prints the app version (e.g., 'todo v0.1.0') and exits with code 0.
When an unknown command is entered and it is within edit distance 2 of a known command, the error message suggests the closest match: 'Error: Unknown command: lst. Did you mean: list?'

The entry point wraps all command execution in a try/except. Unexpected errors print 'Error: An unexpected error occurred: {message}' to stderr and exit with code 1. KeyboardInterrupt (Ctrl+C) exits silently with code 130.

test_cases.name: Unknown command shows error to stderr and help to stdout
todo help produces same output as no arguments
--help and -h flags work
expected_results: Both produce the same help output as `todo help`

test_cases.name: Ctrl+C exits cleanly
steps: Send SIGINT to the process during execution

technical_notes: Entry point: todo.py at project root
Use sys.argv[1] for command, sys.argv[2:] for arguments
Command routing via dict dispatch for extensibility
Keep main() function clean — delegate to handler functions
For 'did you mean' suggestions: use difflib.get_close_matches(cmd, known_commands, n=1, cutoff=0.6)
Wrap everything in try/except Exception for clean error handling; catch KeyboardInterrupt separately
